text.title: Python 3.12/3.13 Modern Patterns
text.summary: Modern Python type hint syntax, asyncio patterns (TaskGroup, timeout, Semaphore), and Python 3.13 feature status.

rule.statement: Use X | None instead of Optional[X] in all new Python code. Use | union syntax instead of Union[X, Y].
rule.rationale: PEP 604 (Python 3.10+) union syntax is shorter and consistent. Mixing old and new styles in one file creates noise and confuses readers.

rule.statement: Use Protocol for structural typing instead of ABC when consumers define the interface, not the implementer.
Use asyncio.TaskGroup (Python 3.11+) for fan-out tasks instead of manual gather + error channels. Wrap with asyncio.timeout to prevent unbounded hangs.
rule.rationale: TaskGroup automatically cancels siblings on first error and propagates exceptions cleanly. Unbounded fan-out without timeout will hang on slow downstream.

example.code:
import asyncio
async def fetch_all(): async with asyncio.timeout(10.0): async with asyncio.TaskGroup() as tg: users = tg.create_task(fetch_users()) products = tg.create_task(fetch_products()) return users.result(), products.result()

rule.statement: Cap async fan-out with asyncio.Semaphore sized to downstream capacity (DB pool, API rate limit), not to CPU count.
Do not use -X nogil or -X jit in production. Free-threaded CPython and JIT are experimental as of Python 3.13.
rule.rationale: These features are not stable for production workloads. The no-GIL mode changes memory model assumptions that many C extensions rely on.
